pages.text: “What do you want to be when you grow up?” asks Teacher Langa.

blocks.text: “What do you want to be when you grow up?” asks Teacher Langa.

pages.text: Story spread 2 Khutso shouts, “I want to be a cook!”

blocks.text: Story spread 2
Khutso shouts, “I want to be a cook!”

pages.text: I love helping my mom bake and cook at home.” Nandi says, “No! That’s a girl’s job. My mom does all the cooking at home.” “Yes he can, Nandi. You can be anything you want to be,” says Teacher Langa. The class chants, “Yes you can!”

blocks.text: I love helping my mom bake and cook at home.”
Nandi says, “No! That’s a girl’s job. My mom does all the cooking at home.”
“Yes he can, Nandi. You can be anything you want to be,” says Teacher Langa.
The class chants, “Yes you can!”

pages.text: Milani says, “I want to build houses like my dad.”

blocks.text: Milani says, “I want to build houses like my dad.”

pages.text: “No you can’t, that’s dangerous!” yells Phathu. “Yes she can, Phathu, as long as she loves it,” replies Teacher Langa. The class chants, “Yes she can!”

blocks.text: “No you can’t, that’s dangerous!” yells Phathu.
“Yes she can, Phathu, as long as she loves it,” replies Teacher Langa.
The class chants, “Yes she can!”

pages.text: Sthelo says, “I want to be a nurse.”

blocks.text: Sthelo says, “I want to be a nurse.”

pages.text: The class bursts out laughing. “What is so funny?” asks Teacher Langa. Nandi replies, “Nurses are girls.” “There are male nurses too,” says Teacher Langa. “So class, can Sthelo be a nurse?” The class booms, “Yes he can!”

blocks.text: The class bursts out laughing.
“What is so funny?” asks Teacher Langa.
Nandi replies, “Nurses are girls.”
“There are male nurses too,” says Teacher Langa.
“So class, can Sthelo be a nurse?”
The class booms, “Yes he can!”

pages.text: “I want to be an artist and draw pretty pictures,’’ says Liyana.

blocks.text: “I want to be an artist and draw pretty pictures,’’ says Liyana.

pages.text: “That’s not a real job,” says Khutso and rolls his eyes. “That sounds lovely, Liyana!” replies teacher Langa. “Can Liyana be an artist?” “Yes she can!” shouts the class!

blocks.text: “That’s not a real job,” says Khutso and rolls his eyes.
“That sounds lovely, Liyana!” replies teacher Langa. “Can Liyana be an artist?”
“Yes she can!” shouts the class!

pages.text: “I want to be an alarm clock,” says Phathu.

blocks.text: “I want to be an alarm clock,” says Phathu.

pages.text: “Huh?” “What do you think, class?” asks Teacher Langa. The class booms, “ Yes you can! Yes you can!”
